chapter_title.en: Chapter 47 – The (Application of) the Henna after the Noura

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/414)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/414)
text: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) وَ قَدْ خَرَجَ مِنَ الْحَمَّامِ وَ هُوَ مِنْ قَرْنِهِ إِلَى قَدَمِهِ مِثْلُ الْوَرْدَةِ مِنْ أَثَرِ الْحِنَّاءِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Ubdous Bin Ibrahim who said,

‘I saw Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and he<sup>asws</sup> had come out from the bathhouse, and he<sup>asws</sup> was like the rose from his<sup>asws</sup> head to his<sup>asws</sup> feet, from the effects of the henna’.
en.sarwar: 4. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ahmad ibn ‘Ubdus ibn Ibrahim who has said the following: “I once saw abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, coming out of the bathhouse and he looked like a flower from his top to his toes because of applying henna.’”
